experiments.hypothesis: We believe shortening the autoplay countdown between episodes from 10 seconds to 5 seconds will increase episodes-per-session because viewers in binge mode are momentarily disengaged by the longer countdown.

experiments.test_name: Pre-roll paywall on free tier — episode 2 vs. episode 5
experiments.hypothesis: We believe moving the upgrade paywall from "after episode 5" to "after episode 2" will accelerate free-to-paid conversion because users will feel investment in the show earlier.
experiments.variant_a: Paywall shown after episode 5 of any series for free-tier users
experiments.variant_b: Paywall shown after episode 2 of any series for free-tier users

experiments.test_name: Continue Watching row order — recency vs. completion likelihood
experiments.hypothesis: We believe ordering Continue Watching by a model-predicted "completion likelihood" instead of by recency will increase resume-watching rate because users are more likely to resume titles they're actually invested in finishing.
experiments.variant_a: Continue Watching row sorted by most recently watched
experiments.variant_b: Continue Watching row sorted by predicted completion likelihood (ML model)
experiments.primary_metric: Resume-watching rate (% of Continue Watching impressions that result in a play)

experiments.test_name: Personalized hero banner — algorithmic vs. editorial
experiments.hypothesis: We believe replacing the editorially-curated hero banner with algorithmically-personalized content will increase click-through to detail pages because each user sees content matched to their taste.
experiments.variant_a: Editorially-curated hero banner (same content for all users in market)
experiments.primary_metric: Click-through rate from homepage to detail page
experiments.guardrail_notes: Time-on-homepage decreased by 11% in Variant B (p < 0.001). Downstream watch-completion rate decreased by 4% in Variant B (p = 0.02).

experiments.test_name: Onboarding personalization quiz — 3 questions vs. 6 questions
experiments.hypothesis: We believe asking new users 6 personalization questions during onboarding (instead of 3) will improve recommendation quality and increase Day-7 retention because the model has more signal to work with.
experiments.primary_metric: Day-7 retention rate (% of new users who return within 7 days)

experiments.test_name: Search bar prominence — embedded vs. floating
experiments.hypothesis: We believe replacing the embedded search bar in the header with a floating search button that expands on tap will increase time-to-first-play because users will discover search more easily when browsing isn't returning what they want.
experiments.variant_a: Search bar embedded in homepage header, always visible
experiments.variant_b: Floating search button (circular, bottom-right) that expands to full-screen search on tap
experiments.context_notes: Search-initiated sessions up 34% in Variant B (p < 0.001). Search-to-play conversion rate up 18% in Variant B (p = 0.003). Long-tail content discovery rate up 22%.

experiments.test_name: Subscription tier comparison page redesign — table vs. card layout
experiments.hypothesis: We believe replacing the comparison table on the upgrade page with a card-based layout will increase upgrade conversion because cards are more scannable and mobile-friendly.
experiments.variant_b: Three vertical cards with featured upgrade highlighted
experiments.context_notes: On Day 11 of the 18-day test, marketing launched a competitor-comparison advertising campaign that drove a different audience segment to the upgrade page. Pre-event (Days 1-10): Variant A 2.4%, Variant B 2.1% (p = 0.18, n.s.). Post-event (Days 11-18): Variant A 2.1%, Variant B 0.6%. The campaign brought in price-sensitive shoppers who responded differently to the layouts.

experiments.test_name: Skip-intro button placement — overlay vs. timeline scrubber
experiments.hypothesis: We believe moving the "Skip Intro" button from a full-screen overlay (current state) to embedded in the timeline scrubber will reduce accidental skips because the scrubber is harder to tap inadvertently than the overlay.
experiments.variant_a: Full-screen "Skip Intro" overlay button (appears 0:10-1:30 of each episode)
experiments.variant_b: "Skip Intro" embedded in the timeline scrubber as a marker
experiments.primary_metric: Skip-intro completion rate (% of episodes where user uses skip)
experiments.segment_notes: Mobile users (n=46,000 per arm): Variant A 71%, Variant B 58% (-18%, p < 0.001). Desktop users (n=18,000 per arm): Variant A 62%, Variant B 71% (+15%, p < 0.001). TV/Connected device users (n=9,000 per arm): Variant A 58%, Variant B 63% (+9%, p = 0.04). The overall "loss" is driven entirely by mobile users where the timeline scrubber is harder to tap.
